idea-plugin.name: Firebase Co-Pilot Build Uploader
idea-plugin.description:
Firebase Co-Pilot: Build Uploader — The all-in-one Firebase App Distribution tool for Android developers.

Build, sign, and distribute your Android app to Firebase App Distribution without ever leaving Android Studio. The plugin handles everything: flavor detection, APK assembly, release signing, upload, release notes, and tester-group distribution — all from a single tool window.

✦ Core Features
  • Auto Flavor Detection — Scans build.gradle / build.gradle.kts for productFlavors (both Groovy and KTS DSLs). Falls back to a firebasecopilot-flavors.json file when Gradle parsing is not possible.
  • Build Type Selection — Supports Debug, Release, and any custom build types defined in your project.
  • One-Click Build & Deploy — Runs ./gradlew :app:assembleFLAVORBUILDTYPE, locates the generated APK, and uploads it to Firebase — all in a single click.
  • Build Only Mode — Assemble an APK locally without uploading.
  • Firebase Auth via Service Account — Authenticates using a Service Account JSON file. No manual token management or CLI setup required.
  • Release Notes — Choose from saved templates or write custom notes. Notes are attached to the Firebase release automatically.
  • Tester Group Distribution — Reads groups from the firebaseAppDistribution { } block in your build.gradle and distributes the release to those groups automatically after upload.
  • Live Build Log — Streams Gradle output directly in the tool window panel.
  • Persistent Settings — Remembers your last App ID, service account path, and up to 20 saved release note templates across IDE restarts.
  • Recent Deployments History — Tracks the last 10 deployments with timestamps, flavors, build types, and Firebase Console links.
  • Auto Project Sync — Re-scans flavors and build types automatically whenever Gradle syncs, keeping the UI always up to date.
✦ Signing Configuration (New in v2.0)
  • Release Signing Warning — Detects missing signing config when "release" build type is selected and prompts you to configure it before building.
  • Signing Config Wizard — Generate a signingConfigs block and inject it into build.gradle / build.gradle.kts automatically.
  • Create New Keystore — Generate a brand-new .jks / .p12 keystore via keytool with a guided form (alias, passwords, certificate DN, validity period) — no terminal required.
  • Use Existing Keystore — Point to an existing keystore file and the plugin writes keystore.properties and injects the Gradle signing block for you.
  • Secure by Default — Credentials are stored in keystore.properties (never hardcoded in build.gradle) and the plugin automatically adds keystore files and property files to .gitignore.
  • Supports Groovy & KTS — Signing config injection works for both build.gradle (Groovy) and build.gradle.kts (Kotlin Script).
✦ How to open
Build → Firebase Co-Pilot: Build Uploader or press Alt+Shift+B. The panel docks to the right sidebar.

✦ Requirements
  • Android Studio (Hedgehog or later recommended)
  • A Firebase project with App Distribution enabled
  • A Service Account JSON with the Firebase App Distribution Admin IAM role
idea-plugin.change-notes:
2.0.2 — Marketplace publishing & validation fixes
  • Fix: Service Account JSON validation — Re-selecting a file (including the same path after the file is restored) now updates the validation label and icons correctly; clearing the path hides success/error icons.
  • Fix: Plugin archive extraction — Plugin ZIP is now built with STORED compression so JetBrains Marketplace can extract and verify the archive.
  • Fix: Android Studio–only compatibility — Declared com.intellij.modules.androidstudio and Gradle plugin dependencies so the plugin is verified for Android Studio, not plain IntelliJ IDEA.
  • Fix: IDE version range — Added explicit since-build / until-build for Android Studio 2025.1 through 2026.2.
2.0.1 — Platform compatibility & security hardening
  • Fix: JetBrains Marketplace compatibility warnings — Migrated away from deprecated IntelliJ Platform APIs scheduled for removal in Android Studio 2025.x / 2026.x (file chooser, Gradle sync listener, and project refresh APIs).
  • Fix: Dependency vulnerabilities — Upgraded Google Auth Library and pinned transitive dependencies to resolve CVE-2023-2976 (Guava) and WS-2019-0379 (Apache Commons Codec).
  • Improvement: Compile SDK — Plugin is now built against Android Studio 2025.1 Platform APIs for forward compatibility with current IDE releases.
  • No functional changes — Build, deploy, signing wizard, keystore creation, and Firebase upload behavior are unchanged from v2.0.0.
2.0.0 — Signed APK & Signing Configuration Support
  • New: Release Signing Wizard — Detects missing signing config when the "release" build type is selected and shows a clickable inline warning. Opens a guided dialog to apply signing configuration directly from the panel.
  • New: Create Keystore dialog — Generate a new .jks / .p12 keystore via keytool without leaving Android Studio. Supports full certificate DN, custom alias, separate key/store passwords, and configurable validity period.
  • New: Gradle signing config injection — Automatically writes keystore.properties and injects a signingConfigs block into build.gradle or build.gradle.kts. Safe to re-apply — replaces any previously injected block.
  • New: Auto .gitignore update — Keystore files and property files are added to .gitignore automatically to prevent accidental credential commits.
  • New: Tester group auto-distribution — Reads groups from the firebaseAppDistribution { } block and distributes releases to those groups after every upload.
  • Fix: Duplicate APK from wrong flavor — APK discovery is now scoped strictly to the selected flavor + build type directory; stale APKs from other flavors are never used.
  • Fix: Gradle abort on missing Firebase task — The -x appDistributionUpload exclusion flag is now only passed when the Firebase Gradle plugin is actually present, preventing "Task not found" errors on plain projects.
  • Fix: Correct Firebase Console URL — The console link now uses the URL returned directly by the Firebase API instead of a reconstructed one.
  • Improvement: Auto-refresh on Gradle sync — Flavor and build type combos update automatically after every Gradle sync without requiring a manual refresh.
1.0.0 — Initial release
  • Auto flavor detection from build.gradle / build.gradle.kts
  • Build type selection with flavor combos
  • One-click Build & Deploy to Firebase App Distribution
  • Service Account JSON authentication
  • Release notes templates with persistent history
  • Live Gradle build log in the tool window

action.description: Open Firebase Co-Pilot: Build Uploader panel to build, sign, and distribute your Android app
